title: rubus × infecundus
intro: Rubus × infecundus, also known as the sterile blackberry, is a sterile hybrid of the Rubus genus that is characterized by its inability to produce viable seeds due to its triploid condition. The medicinal properties of Rubus × infecundus include its high content of anthocyanins and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Rubus × infecundus can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or jam, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
